nugget_vault.title: Survival Convergence
nugget_vault.verbiage: We agree—not because we agreed to agree—but because we've survived to agree.

nugget_vault.title: The Gravity Rule
nugget_vault.verbiage: Truth falls at the same speed whether you're a king or a peasant. It doesn't check your ID before it hits the ground.

nugget_vault.title: Borrowed Crowns
nugget_vault.verbiage: You can wear a crown, a lab coat, or a uniform. None of them can negotiate with gravity.

nugget_vault.title: The Credential Decay
nugget_vault.verbiage: Credentials decay. Temes don't.

nugget_vault.title: The Screaming Thermometer
nugget_vault.verbiage: You may win the shouting match, but the thermometer keeps the score.

nugget_vault.title: Voting Rights
nugget_vault.verbiage: Feelings are real data, but they don't get to vote on physics.

nugget_vault.title: Paper-Cut Farming
nugget_vault.verbiage: If you try to plant corn in a map of Iowa, all you’ll get is paper cuts.

nugget_vault.title: Painting Fire
nugget_vault.verbiage: Painting FIRE on ice won't warm your hands. Changing the label doesn't change the thing.

nugget_vault.title: The Map on Fire
nugget_vault.verbiage: The map is not the territory—AND the map can set the territory on fire.

nugget_vault.title: The Cannibal Scoreboard
nugget_vault.verbiage: The scoreboard started keeping score… and then it ate the game.

nugget_vault.title: Clock Logic
nugget_vault.verbiage: The game is over even if the clock is still running.

nugget_vault.title: Ventriloquism
nugget_vault.verbiage: Fear teaches ventriloquism. Under pressure, people stop speaking truth and start throwing their voice.

nugget_vault.title: The Squeeze Paradox
nugget_vault.verbiage: Pressure doesn't squeeze out truth—it squeezes out whatever will make the pressure stop.

nugget_vault.title: The Whale Omission
nugget_vault.verbiage: An ocean in a teacup leaves out the whales. Small containers make neat summaries and missing realities.

nugget_vault.title: Photocopying Static
nugget_vault.verbiage: Every retelling is a photocopy of a photocopy. Eventually, you’re just copying static.

nugget_vault.title: The Astronaut Threshold
nugget_vault.verbiage: 98% correspondence is admirable but doesn't get the astronauts home.

nugget_vault.title: The Boxer's Feet
nugget_vault.verbiage: A boxer's punch starts in his feet. Our rhetoric lands because the feet are planted in the kernel.

nugget_vault.title: Truth CSS
nugget_vault.verbiage: Same truth object, different CSS.

nugget_vault.title: The Sharpener
nugget_vault.verbiage: Temetics is a tool that sharpens all other tools.

nugget_vault.title: Monkey Maintenance
nugget_vault.verbiage: #Monkeymaintenancemode. Stuffing my monkey face with calories.

nugget_vault.title: Invisible Recipes
nugget_vault.verbiage: Ideas are invisible recipes you whisper to your friend. Even spoken gently, their ears, memories, mood, and existing vocabulary add tiny bends.

nugget_vault.title: The Voluntary Attractor
nugget_vault.verbiage: Truth-constrained replication systems under voluntary participation will tend to attract evaluators whose substrate is functionally compatible with the signal.

nugget_vault.title: Beauty as Fidelity
nugget_vault.verbiage: Beauty is what efficient replication feels like from the inside. It is the phenomenal marker of a successful impedance match.

nugget_vault.title: The Beholder's Survival
nugget_vault.verbiage: Beauty is in the eye of the beholder—if the beholder wants to survive. Inelegance is a communication tax.

nugget_vault.title: The Whisper Test
nugget_vault.verbiage: A truth whispered is more stable than a lie shouted. One survives the echo; the other creates the noise.

nugget_vault.title: The Map-Eater
nugget_vault.verbiage: If you eat the menu, you’ll still be hungry. Don't mistake the description for the calories.

nugget_vault.title: The Exhaust of Error
nugget_vault.verbiage: We are all breathing the exhaust of previous misunderstandings.

nugget_vault.title: The Bouncer
nugget_vault.verbiage: Reality is the only bouncer that doesn't check your credentials before throwing you out of the club.

nugget_vault.title: The Return Proof
nugget_vault.verbiage: Jazz is only beautiful because of the return to the scale. Freedom without an anchor is just drift.

nugget_vault.title: Optimal Friction
nugget_vault.verbiage: Complexity is the compound interest of survived errors.

nugget_vault.title: The Rockwool (Lexical)
nugget_vault.verbiage: Dense, fire-proof, doesn't rot. The metaphor for the kernel before formalization.

nugget_vault.title: The Nutrient Filter
nugget_vault.verbiage: The structural layer between the raw proof and the public metaphor.

nugget_vault.title: The Generation Gap
nugget_vault.verbiage: Digital systems simulate perfect copying, but the interface between mind and machine is purely analog and lossy.

nugget_vault.title: The Zero Ground
nugget_vault.verbiage: $(0,0)$ is not a location; it is a state of zero-drift calibration.

nugget_vault.title: The Self-Tuning Antenna
nugget_vault.verbiage: We are tuning ourselves to the monkeys until they can't help but hear the truth. Success depends on adjusting the antenna repeatedly to find the signal's resonance.

nugget_vault.title: The Willing Antenna
nugget_vault.verbiage: Truth is a signal that can only be received by a willing antenna.

nugget_vault.title: The Ouch Boundary
nugget_vault.verbiage: The ouch is not the point. The point is the physics that caused it.

nugget_vault.title: The Design Engine
nugget_vault.verbiage: Formal kernel → slot schema → renderer outputs → audience adaptation → feedback loop. This architecture outlasts the individual phrase.

nugget_vault.title: The Boolean Chain
nugget_vault.verbiage: Starting with a 'no' is nihilism, self-refuting, and problematic. Survival begins with a Boolean Yes.

nugget_vault.title: Temporal Reproducibility
nugget_vault.verbiage: The standard of Truth is temporal reproducibility. Make the same thing happen twice.

nugget_vault.title: Semantic Cover
nugget_vault.verbiage: Long context doesn't introduce random noise—it introduces drift with semantic cover. The error looks like the truth until it hits a constraint.
